description:
## Goal

Add a `/update-helixir` slash command (Claude Code skill) that lets users update their helixir MCP server to the latest version from npm in one step — mirroring the `update-plugin` command in protoLabs Studio.

## Background

helixir is installed globally via npm (e.g., `npm install -g helixir` or `npx helixir`). When a new version ships, users currently have no easy way to update inside a Claude Code session. This command closes that gap.

## Implementation

Create a new skill file at `src/skills/update-helixir.md` (or wherever helixir's skills/commands live — research the project structure first).

The skill should:

1. **Detect install method** — Check how helixir is currently installed:
   - `npm list -g helixir` — global npm install
   - `which helixir` — check if it's on PATH
   - Read current version from the installed package

2. **Check latest version on npm**:
   ```bash
   npm view helixir version
   ```

3. **Compare versions** — If already on latest, tell the user and exit cleanly.

4. **Run the update**:
   ```bash
   npm install -g helixir@latest
   ```

5. **Verify** — Re-check installed version after update to confirm it changed.

6. **Tell the user to restart Claude Code** — The MCP server won't reload until Claude Code restarts. Print a clear message:
   > "helixir updated to vX.Y.Z. Please restart Claude Code for the new version to take effect."

## Reference

See how `update-plugin` is structured in protoLabs Studio:
`/Volumes/Development/protoMaker/packages/mcp-server/plugins/automaker/commands/update-plugin.md`

The helixir version is much simpler — no `.env` migration, no plugin registry, no hooks. Just: check → update → verify → tell user to restart.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `/update-helixir` skill exists and is registered
- [ ] Detects current installed version
- [ ] Checks latest version from npm registry
- [ ] Skips update if already on latest (with confirmation message)
- [ ] Runs `npm install -g helixir@latest` when update needed
- [ ] Verifies version after install
- [ ] Clearly instructs user to restart Claude Code
- [ ] Works whether helixir is installed globally or via npx

## Notes

- This feature should be implemented AFTER the current dev→staging→main promotion completes (it will be part of the next release cycle)
- Keep it simple — this is a skill file (markdown), not a code change to the MCP server itself
title: feat: add /update-helixir slash command to self-update from npm

coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 1** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> .coderabbit.yaml (1)
> > `24-25`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **Move `path_filters` to the correct schema location.** > > `path_filters` must be defined directly under `reviews`, not under `reviews.auto_review`. The current placement violates the CodeRabbit schema and may cause the filter to be ignored. > >
> 🔧 Proposed fix > > ```diff > reviews: > profile: 'assertive' > request_changes_workflow: true > high_level_summary: true > poem: false > review_status: true > collapse_walkthrough: false > + path_filters: > + - '!**/AUDIT.md' > auto_review: > enabled: true > drafts: false > base_branches: > - main > - dev > - staging > ignore_title_keywords: > - 'AUDIT' > - 'audit' > - 'Deep Audit' > - 'deep-audit' > - path_filters: > - - '!**/AUDIT.md' > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In @.coderabbit.yaml around lines 24 - 25, Move the path_filters key out of the > nested reviews.auto_review block and place it directly under the top-level > reviews mapping; specifically locate the path_filters entry and the > reviews.auto_review block (symbols: path_filters, reviews, auto_review) and > relocate the path_filters array so it is a direct child of reviews, preserving > its values and YAML indentation so it conforms to the CodeRabbit schema. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each find
